custom_scanner.description: Synthetic plugin extension used for demo-mode report completeness.

report.summary: The demo plugin flagged one additional secrets notebook and enriched two findings with analyst labels.
report.title: Custom Scanner Insights

findings.recommendation: Rotate the exposed token immediately and remove all plaintext notebook copies from the device.
findings.summary: A staging integration token is present in a locally synced markdown notebook.
findings.title: GitHub token pattern detected

findings.recommendation: Revoke the key, replace it with a managed secret, and clean the runbook export.
findings.summary: A cloud automation key is staged in a deployment runbook export.
findings.title: AWS access key pattern detected

findings.recommendation: Invalidate the key material and remove the paste artifact from local storage.
findings.summary: An SSH private key snippet is embedded in a troubleshooting paste.
findings.title: Private key material detected

findings.recommendation: Move the export into controlled storage and restrict local copies.
findings.summary: An incident export contains leadership and finance addresses in plaintext.
findings.title: Plaintext email address collection detected

findings.recommendation: Force password reset, require MFA re-validation, and review active sessions for the identity cluster.
findings.summary: finance.ops@example.com appears across four staged breach collections with credential reuse overlap.
findings.title: Identity exposure detected in breach intelligence

findings.recommendation: Rotate the related service credentials and review access logs for replay activity.
findings.summary: payments.portal@example.com appears in multiple credential leak aggregations.
findings.title: Identity exposure detected in breach intelligence

findings.recommendation: Break the reuse immediately and enforce a unique-password baseline across the service set.
findings.summary: Three staged critical services share the same password candidate.
findings.title: Password reuse detected

findings.recommendation: Replace the common password with a long unique passphrase.
findings.summary: One demo credential candidate remains present in a common-password list.
findings.title: Common password detected

findings.recommendation: Remove names, aliases, or business terms from password construction.
findings.summary: A staged password includes the user alias, making it easier to guess.
findings.title: Password contains user identifier

findings.recommendation: Tighten file permissions and remove the backup from collaborative folders.
findings.summary: An SSH private key backup is readable by a broad local group.
findings.title: Sensitive file has broad permissions

findings.recommendation: Delete the export and use approved vault storage for any required credentials.
findings.summary: A browser export with session material remains in a downloads subfolder.
findings.title: Credential store artifact detected

findings.recommendation: Re-enable the protection settings and review the profile for unauthorized changes.
findings.summary: Safe browsing protections are disabled in the analyst's default browser profile.
findings.title: Disabled browser protection setting detected

findings.recommendation: Remove stored logins from the browser and move them into a managed password manager.
findings.summary: The default profile retains saved business credentials.
findings.title: Saved logins present in browser profile

findings.recommendation: Review and remove unnecessary extensions to reduce extension-supply-chain risk.
findings.summary: The analyst profile contains more extensions than the defined hardening baseline.
findings.title: High browser extension count detected

findings.recommendation: Quarantine the message, warn the target team, and validate recent reply activity.
findings.summary: A staged executive escalation email redirects replies to an external mailbox.
findings.title: Reply-To mismatch detected in email headers

findings.recommendation: Block the sender infrastructure and review message delivery controls.
findings.summary: The staged message includes failed authentication signals consistent with impersonation.
findings.title: Email authentication failure detected

findings.recommendation: Block the URL and notify all users named in the targeted distribution.
findings.summary: The message links to a fake executive review portal with urgent payment language.
findings.title: Suspicious URLs detected in email body

findings.recommendation: Block the URL immediately and investigate whether any user interacted with the destination.
findings.summary: The staged executive review URL is tagged malicious by multiple threat feeds.
findings.title: Threat intelligence match for url

findings.recommendation: Block the domain and add it to mail and web security controls.
findings.summary: The impersonation domain is tied to prior business-email compromise campaigns.
findings.title: Threat intelligence match for domain

findings.recommendation: Block the resolved IP where practical and review historical network contact.
findings.summary: The staged phishing infrastructure resolves to an IP associated with prior credential-harvesting activity.
findings.title: Threat intelligence match for ip

findings.recommendation: Treat the correlated findings as one incident cluster and prioritize identity containment before broader cleanup.
findings.summary: The staged environment shows multiple independent paths to account takeover and business-email compromise.
findings.title: Compounded identity takeover pattern detected

recommended_actions: Rotate exposed tokens, cloud keys, and all reused credentials immediately.
Enable or verify MFA on impacted identities and review active sessions.
Quarantine the phishing campaign, block the malicious URL, domain, and IP, and alert affected users.
Re-enable browser protections and remove saved logins from analyst workstations.
metadata.risk_explanation: Any single control failure would be concerning, but the combination of breach overlap, reusable secrets, and malicious delivery infrastructure indicates a highly actionable account-takeover and business-email-compromise scenario.
metadata.summary: The demo environment now reflects a clear identity-security incident with exposed tokens, breach hits, credential reuse, malicious phishing infrastructure, and browser hardening gaps.

suspicious_patterns.summary: Multiple business identities appear in breach datasets while the service set still shows password reuse.
suspicious_patterns.title: Credential reuse plus breach exposure

suspicious_patterns.summary: The executive impersonation email overlaps with malicious URL, domain, and infrastructure intelligence.
suspicious_patterns.title: Phishing with malicious infrastructure

suspicious_patterns.summary: Local token exposure, cloud key leakage, and browser-stored credentials create multiple paths to credential replay and session hijack.
suspicious_patterns.title: Exposed cloud and session material raises takeover risk

notes: Demo mode generated synthetic findings. No live scan was executed.
This staged incident profile is safe to publish and intended for screenshots, walkthroughs, and portfolio demos.

Rotate the exposed token immediately and remove all plaintext notebook copies from the device.
Force password reset, require MFA re-validation, and review active sessions for the identity cluster.

Block the domain and add it to mail and web security controls.
